entries.formulaBefore: maxDDpct was copied into both volatility and maximum drawdown
entries.formulaAfter: sample standard deviation (N-1) of annual portfolio returns, expressed in percent; no additional annualization factor
entries.expectedDirection: not monotonic; volatility and drawdown become independent risk measures

entries.formulaBefore: absolute count of successful years with cutPct >= 10, including accumulation years
entries.formulaAfter: completed decumulation years with finite cutPct >= 10 divided by completed decumulation years with a finite cut decision; denominator zero is nullable
entries.expectedDirection: unit and denominator change; old and new values are not directly comparable

entries.formulaBefore: household care triggers were assigned to P1 and absent person entries were stored as age zero
entries.formulaAfter: P1 and P2 use separate observed-entry counters over requested runs; conditional person distributions exclude non-entry sentinels and expose sampleSize plus missingness
entries.expectedDirection: person rates and conditional medians may move independently; empty distributions change from zero to null

entries.formulaBefore: DepotStart minus DepotEnd was labelled as depot-financed care cost and annual household spend used max(P1 target, P2 target)
entries.formulaAfter: modelled annual care additions passed to the simulation are summed as P1 plus P2, accumulated per person and household, and reported in real EUR at simulation-start prices; nominal path fields use the NominalEur suffix
entries.expectedDirection: values are not comparable because attribution, household addition and price basis change

entries.formulaBefore: median with care minus median without care despite the opposite UI description
entries.formulaAfter: real-EUR median without care minus real-EUR median with care, explicitly marked as an unpaired non-causal group comparison
entries.expectedDirection: sign reverses relative to the old implementation

entries.formulaBefore: technical errors were separated, but financial terminal reasons were not exposed as an exhaustive inventory and the UI derived a generic success rate from failCount
entries.formulaAfter: each requested run is exactly ruin, all_dead, horizon_exhausted or technical_error; floor coverage is (all_dead + horizon_exhausted) / requestedRuns only for technical-error-free batches and otherwise null
entries.expectedDirection: headline becomes nullable on technical error and censored horizon paths become explicitly visible

entries.formulaBefore: duration and depletion age used Uint8 with sentinel 255; Monte Carlo used 0.0005 outside the mortality table while Sweep used 1
entries.formulaAfter: duration and age use Uint32 plus explicit missingness; all simulator paths use probability 1 outside the mortality table and reject age-plus-horizon overflow
entries.expectedDirection: buffer storage increases by 7 bytes per run; valid in-range paths are unchanged while out-of-range mortality fails closed consistently
